text.title: Kubernetes Basics Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules plus a skip-clause for `Kubernetes Basics`. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Every object MUST carry app.kubernetes.io/name, app.kubernetes.io/instance, app.kubernetes.io/component labels.
rule.rationale: Recommended-labels are the only stable selector mechanism for tooling (Helm, ArgoCD, k9s).

rule.statement: Every container MUST declare readinessProbe AND livenessProbe; pods without probes are forbidden for production.

rationale.source: Kubernetes probe docs, Google SRE workbook ch. 12

rule.rationale: Without readinessProbe traffic hits unhealthy pods; without livenessProbe deadlocked pods stay forever.

rule.statement: Container securityContext MUST set runAsNonRoot true and runAsUser non-zero; root containers are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Root-in-container is the dominant container escape vector; non-root closes the easy path.

rule.statement: Every container MUST declare CPU + memory requests (limits optional but recommended); requests=0 is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Without requests the scheduler picks arbitrary nodes; noisy-neighbour OOMs follow.

rule.statement: Container image MUST reference an immutable tag (digest or fixed version); latest is forbidden.
rule.rationale: latest makes rollbacks impossible and breaks reproducibility.

rule.statement: If Applies If preconditions fail OR any Skip If trigger fires, do not run this methodology.
